Description: An intrinsically disordered protein that shields DNA from ionizing radiation and ROS.
Ported To: Human cells, mice, C. elegans, yeast, plants
Potential Human Enhancement: Radiotolerance for space travel and protection during radiotherapy

Description: Produces high molecular weight hyaluronic acid linked to cancer resistance in naked mole rats.
Potential Human Enhancement: Lifespan extension and strong tumor suppression

Description: Cold‑inducible RNA‑binding protein improving DNA repair and stress tolerance.
Potential Human Enhancement: Better tolerance to cold stress and improved genome maintenance

Description: 3'→5' exonuclease that degrades cytosolic DNA and contributes to genome stability.
Potential Human Enhancement: Reduced inflammatory DNA signaling and improved genome maintenance

Source Organism: Naked Mole Rat / Long-lived species (Heterocephalus glaber)
Description: Mitochondrial superoxide dismutase detoxifying superoxide radicals.
Potential Human Enhancement: Protection against oxidative damage and improved mitochondrial health

Description: Longevity‑associated transcription factor regulating stress resistance and metabolism.
Potential Human Enhancement: Improved stress resistance and lifespan‑associated metabolic regulation

Description: Anti‑aging protein regulating phosphate metabolism and insulin signaling.
Potential Human Enhancement: Lifespan extension and improved cognitive resilience

Description: Elephants possess multiple TP53 copies providing strong tumor suppression.
Potential Human Enhancement: Enhanced tumor suppression mechanisms

Description: DNA repair enzyme removing O6‑alkylguanine lesions.
Potential Human Enhancement: Resistance to mutagenic chemicals and DNA damage

Description: Catalytic component of telomerase maintaining telomere length.
Potential Human Enhancement: Extended cellular lifespan and delayed tissue aging

Description: Aquaporin channel enabling rapid water transport across membranes.
Potential Human Enhancement: Improved hydration regulation and endurance

Source Organism: Tibetan Highlanders (Denisovan introgression into Homo sapiens)
Description: Hypoxia‑inducible factor variant associated with Tibetan high‑altitude adaptation.
Potential Human Enhancement: Enhanced oxygen utilization at high altitude

Description: A cytoplasmic-abundant heat-soluble protein that forms protective hydrogels to stabilize biomolecules during total water loss.
Potential Human Enhancement: Cellular survival under extreme dehydration, high osmotic stress resilience, and dry-preservation of human blood or tissues.

Description: Regulatory and structural proteins that detect DNA damage and stimulate extreme post-radiation genome reassembly. Survives 5000 Gy — its genome shatters into hundreds of fragments and reassembles perfectly in hours.
Potential Human Enhancement: Near-instantaneous genome reconstitution following lethal radiation. The biological undo button.

Description: Melanin pigments that absorb ionizing gamma radiation and convert it into metabolic energy via radiosynthesis. These fungi grow TOWARD radiation inside Chernobyl's reactor and have been tested on the ISS as living radiation shielding.
Potential Human Enhancement: Conversion of harmful gamma radiation into usable biological energy — radiation as food. Tested as living shielding for Mars missions.

Description: ~20 copies of tumour suppressor retrogenes that induce hyper-aggressive apoptosis in damaged cells. Elephants almost never get cancer despite massive cell count.
Potential Human Enhancement: Massive redundancy in cancer surveillance, drastically reducing lifetime cancer incidence.

Description: 81 duplicated genes enriched for double-strand DNA break repair, a unique p53 insertion, and expanded NF-kB signalling families. The longest-lived vertebrate (~400 years).
Potential Human Enhancement: Centuries-scale genomic maintenance, enhanced tumour suppression, reduced chronic inflammation.

Description: A POT1 variant reducing telomerase inhibition, upregulated sirtuin SIRT3, and telomere regulator RTEL1 — enabling whole-body transdifferentiation from adult back to juvenile polyp.
Ported To: N/A (POT1 validated in human recombinant protein)
Potential Human Enhancement: Cellular age-reversal via transdifferentiation, enhanced telomere maintenance — cyclical rejuvenation instead of linear aging.

Description: Planarian neoblasts — adult pluripotent stem cells comprising ~20% of body cells — are maintained by piwi-family genes (smedwi-1/2/3). Cut a planarian into 200+ pieces and each fragment regenerates a complete organism with brain and eyes.
Potential Human Enhancement: A body saturated with pluripotent stem cells capable of rebuilding any organ from scratch. Whole-body regeneration from fragments. The ultimate biological backup system.

Description: An RNA-binding protein that reprograms cellular metabolism via let-7 miRNA suppression, reactivating embryonic regeneration pathways — blastema formation, scarless wound healing, full limb regrowth.
Potential Human Enhancement: Reactivation of limb regeneration, scarless healing, and metabolic reprogramming for tissue repair.

Description: A serine substitution at position 358 in STING that dampens interferon overactivation, plus complete loss of the PYHIN inflammasome family. Bats coexist with Ebola, SARS, Nipah without disease.
Potential Human Enhancement: Coexistence with lethal viruses without immunopathology, prevention of cytokine storms. Immune resilience without suppression.

Description: 10-fold elevation in muscle myoglobin (O2 storage), 25-fold cardiac glutathione (antioxidant), and seal-specific miRNAs for hypoxia tolerance. Dives 900 m for 60+ minutes on one breath.
Potential Human Enhancement: Radical extension of breath-hold duration, tolerance of profound hypoxia without organ damage, built-in reperfusion protection.

Description: Ice-binding proteins that adhere to ice crystal planes, preventing lethal intracellular freezing. Molecular antifreeze operating below the freezing point of blood.
Ported To: Mouse ovaries, salmon, Drosophila, wheat, tobacco
Potential Human Enhancement: Survival under deep hypothermia, flawless organ cryopreservation, total frostbite resistance.

Description: Cetacean-specific mutations in circadian clock genes CLOCK and BMAL1 that enhance wake-promoting transcription, enabling one hemisphere to sleep while the other stays alert. Dolphins never fully lose consciousness.
Potential Human Enhancement: Half-brain sleep — resting one hemisphere while the other is fully alert. Halved sleep needs, perpetual partial consciousness.

Description: The cochlear motor protein prestin underwent identical parallel amino acid substitutions (notably N7T) in both echolocating bats and toothed whales — a textbook case of convergent molecular evolution. These changes increase voltage sensitivity of outer hair cells, enabling ultrasonic hearing up to 200 kHz.
Potential Human Enhancement: Biological sonar — emitting ultrasonic pulses and interpreting echoes to build a 3D spatial map in total darkness. Navigation, object detection, and spatial awareness without light.

Description: A heat-activated calcium channel tuned to detect temperature changes as small as 0.003 C. A biological infrared camera overlaid on normal vision.
Potential Human Enhancement: Seeing heat as an image in total darkness. Night vision without equipment, disease detection by temperature mapping.

Description: A massively expanded opsin family producing 16 spectral photoreceptor classes and circular polarisation vision. The most complex eye in the animal kingdom.
Potential Human Enhancement: Perception of UV, polarisation patterns, and spectral channels invisible to human trichromacy. Detecting camouflage and toxins in a vastly richer colour space.

Description: A retinal cryptochrome generating magnetically sensitive radical pairs via four flavin-tryptophan electron transfers. A quantum biological compass. Expression rises during migration.
Potential Human Enhancement: Built-in biological compass — sensing Earth's magnetic field as a visual overlay. Quantum biology meets wayfinding.

Description: A reflective layer behind the retina composed of precisely arranged guanine nanocrystals that bounce unabsorbed photons back through photoreceptors for a second pass. Doubles photon capture efficiency in dim light. The reason cat eyes glow in the dark.
Potential Human Enhancement: Dramatically enhanced low-light vision by doubling photon capture — seeing clearly in near-total darkness. The retinal backlight. Eyes that glow.

Description: A cephalopod-specific protein (reflectin) that self-assembles into tunable thin-film nanostructures in iridophore cells, combined with neurally controlled pigment-filled chromatophore organs that expand/contract in milliseconds. Together they produce instant full-spectrum colour and pattern change across the entire body.
Potential Human Enhancement: Instant skin colour and pattern change — real-time adaptive camouflage, emotional display via skin, or aesthetic self-expression. A living display surface.

Description: A 238-amino-acid protein that absorbs blue/UV light and re-emits it as bright green fluorescence. Requires no cofactors or substrates — just fold it and it glows. Already the most widely used reporter gene in biology.
Ported To: Bacteria, yeast, plants, mice, zebrafish, human cells, cats, pigs, monkeys
Potential Human Enhancement: Glowing skin, glowing tattoos, glowing hair — fluorescent self-expression visible under UV. Biological glow-in-the-dark. Also: real-time visualisation of gene expression, disease markers, or metabolic states readable through the skin.

Description: An enzyme (luciferase) that catalyses oxidation of a small molecule (luciferin) to produce cold bioluminescent light with near-perfect quantum efficiency (~41%). No external light source needed — the organism IS the light source.
Ported To: Bacteria, yeast, plants, human cells, mice
Potential Human Enhancement: Self-generated visible light without any external energy input — glowing in the dark, not just under UV. Biological flashlights, illuminated blood vessels, glowing wounds that signal infection. The body as a lantern.

Description: A duplicated voltage-gated sodium channel (NaV1.4a) massively upregulated in electrocytes stacked 5000-6000 deep to generate 860 V. Burns metabolic fuel to make electricity.
Potential Human Enhancement: Biological phone charging (burns fat for free), neural stimulation, wound healing, or bioelectronic device powering. The body as a battery.

Description: Steals intact chloroplasts from algae and runs them in its own gut cells for up to 10 months. Contains the algal gene psbO and produces polyketide sunscreens to keep stolen chloroplasts alive. The only animal that photosynthesises.
Ported To: N/A (draft genome; psbO detected in adult tissue)
Potential Human Enhancement: An animal that eats sunlight. Supplementary energy from captured chloroplasts, reducing caloric needs and producing oxygen. The ultimate biological solar panel.

Description: A massively expanded family of 72 corneous beta-proteins (CBPs) that polymerise into microscopic setae — hair-like fibres tipped with spatulae that exploit van der Waals forces for dry adhesion to any surface including polished glass. No glue needed.
Potential Human Enhancement: Dry adhesion to any surface — walls, ceilings, glass, wet or dry. Stick your phone to your arm while jogging. Climb things. Gecko grip without gecko feet.
